activities_projects.title: MLA Word Search
activities_projects.objective: Learn the full form and basic meaning of MLA
steps: Find the full form of MLA (Member of Legislative Assembly) in the book
Write down who elects an MLA (the people)
Identify if an MLA belongs to a political party
List one thing an MLA does for their area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that MLAs are elected representatives of the people
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the full form and election process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: My Constituency Map
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of a constituency
steps: Look at a map of your state showing different divisions
Identify your own area/district on the map
Label it as your 'Constituency'
Color your constituency and neighboring ones in different colors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes how a state is divided into smaller units for elections
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling and coloring of the constituency area (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Majority Game
activities_projects.objective: Understand how a government is formed
steps: Imagine a state has 20 seats. Party A has 11, Party B has 9
Group the buttons to show which party has 'more than half'
Identify the 'Majority' party as the one that forms the government
Label the other party as the 'Opposition'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns the basic rule for forming a ruling government
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the ruling and opposition parties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Chief Minister's Name Card
activities_projects.objective: Identify the head of the state government
steps: Find out the name of the current Chief Minister (CM) of your state
Write it in big letters on a card
Draw a small symbol of their political party next to the name
Write 'Leader of the Ruling Party' below the name
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Knows the current executive head of their state government
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the name and party details (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: A Day in the Assembly: Roleplay
steps: Arrange chairs like the Legislative Assembly (semi-circle)
One student acts as the 'Speaker' sitting at the front
Other students act as MLAs asking questions about 'clean drinking water'
One student as 'Minister' answers what the government is doing
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes how the assembly works through questions and answers
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and use of key terms like 'Speaker' and 'Minister' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Patalpuram Problem List
activities_projects.objective: Follow the case study of a public health issue
steps: Read the comic strip about Patalpuram in the chapter
List two problems the people faced (e.g., diarrhea, no water)
Identify what the people did to show their anger (rally/protest)
Write down what the CM promised to do
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes how public problems lead to government action
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of the problem and solution list from the text (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Government Departments Scrapbook
activities_projects.objective: Identify different branches of the government
steps: Find names of government departments (e.g., Health, Education, Agriculture)
Paste a picture related to each (e.g., a school for Education)
Write one simple sentence on what that department does
Label them as 'State Government Departments'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Realizes that the government works through many different offices
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct naming and description of at least three departments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Governor's Stamp
activities_projects.objective: Learn the role of the Governor
steps: Draw a seal or stamp that says 'Governor of the State'
Write that the Governor is the 'Head of the State'
Note that the Governor is appointed by the Central Government
Write one job of the Governor (e.g., signing a bill to make it a law)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between the elected CM and the appointed Governor
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the Governor's appointment and status (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Press Conference Reporter
steps: Imagine you are a journalist at a press conference
Write two questions you would ask the Health Minister about hospitals
Write down how the Minister might respond using 'Government Action'
Discuss why newspapers are important to read about the government
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns about the role of media in a democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of questions and relevance to government work (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Wall Newspaper Activity
steps: Pick an issue like 'Playground Maintenance' or 'School Toilets'
Interview two classmates about their opinion on the issue
Write a short paragraph on 'What the Government should do'
Paste it on the classroom wall for others to read
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Encourages students to think about public issues and solutions
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of the issue presented (10 marks)
